name: Maceda – Loop via Baños de Molgas
name_original: Maceda – vuelta por Baños de Molgas
description: Explore the Maceda – tour around Baños de Molgas, a moderate 15.8-mile touring cycling route with 1076 feet of gain and historic sights.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Maceda traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, deep valleys, and extensive forests of ancient trees. The region is situated amidst mountains and rivers, offering diverse terrain for cyclists. Notable features include the dramatic Sil River Canyon with its granite walls and the scenic Arnoia River, providing varied cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Maceda The most popular touring cycling route is Maceda – Loop via Baños de Molgas, a 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 39 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the local countryside and small villages. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cachamuíña Reservoir – Cachamuíña Reservoir loop from Boeiros, a moderate 15.5 miles (25.0 km) path. This route circles the reservoir, providing scenic waterside cycling. Local touring cyclists also love the Allariz loop from Estación de Ponte Ambía, a 47.1 miles (75.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 8 minutes. Touring cycling around Maceda is defined by dramatic river canyons, ancient forests, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Maceda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 65 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Maceda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Maceda?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes available around Maceda, offering a variety of experiences. These include 9 moderate routes and 16 more challenging options for experienced cyclists.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Maceda?
A: Maceda benefits from a temperate oceanic climate, making it suitable for touring cycling throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and conditions for exploring the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Maceda?
A: The terrain around Maceda is characterized by rolling hills, deep valleys, and extensive forests. You'll find a mix of quiet rural roads, some gravel paths, and routes that traverse dramatic river canyons like the Sil River. Expect varied and engaging cycling experiences with both ascents and descents.

Q: Are there options for different skill levels among the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, Maceda offers routes for various skill levels. You can find moderate loops, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017187767/maceda-loop-via-banos-de-molgas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maceda – Loop via Baños de Molgas&lt;/a&gt;, which is 15.8 miles (25.4 km) long. For those seeking more challenge, there are 16 difficult routes with significant elevation gain, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149999" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Allariz loop from Estación de Ponte Ambía&lt;/a&gt;, a 47.1 miles (75.9 km) trail.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I discover along touring cycling routes in Maceda?
A: Many routes offer access to breathtaking viewpoints, especially within the Ribeira Sacra and Sil River Canyon. Notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3726234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Boqueiriño Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3726236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cividade Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4607311" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Coto das Boedas Meander from Vilouxe&lt;/a&gt;, which provide panoramic vistas of the canyons and terraced vineyards.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to visit while cycling?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in historical sites. You can visit the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3136768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Pedro de Rocas&lt;/a&gt;, partially excavated into rock, or explore the medieval Maceda Castle. The Santa María Parish Church is another fine example of Galician Romanesque architecture. The 'Ruta dos Muíños' (Route of the Mills) also offers a glimpse into local history with its old hydraulic mills.

Q: What natural features are prominent on the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes showcase dramatic natural features such as the deep Sil River Canyon with its granite walls, extensive forests of ancient chestnut and oak trees, and the scenic Arnoia River. For those seeking mountainous terrain, the Sierra de San Mamede offers challenging ascents and expansive views.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Maceda?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Maceda are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cachamuíña Reservoir – Cachamuíña Reservoir loop from Boeiros&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic waterside cycling, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40014219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Mao Canyon – Mao Walkways loop from Parada de Sil&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is public transport available to access touring cycling routes in Maceda?
A: While Maceda is a rural area, some public transport options may connect to larger towns nearby. For specific routes, it's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to a starting point, as many routes begin in or near villages with limited public transport access.

Q: What are the parking options near the touring cycling routes?
A: Most touring cycling routes in Maceda start from towns or villages where public parking is generally available. For routes beginning in more remote areas, look for designated parking spots or inquire locally. It's always a good idea to plan your starting point and check for parking availability beforehand.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Maceda?
A: While many routes involve rolling hills, some sections along the Arnoia River or specific local paths might be suitable for families. For easier, less strenuous options, look for routes described as 'moderate' or those that stick to flatter riverbanks, though dedicated family-specific routes might be limited.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Maceda?
A: The touring cycling routes in Maceda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 65 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Maceda's varied terrain, often praising the dramatic river canyons, ancient forests, and the diverse cycling experiences available.

Q: Where can I find refreshments or places to eat near the routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near small villages and towns where you can find local cafes, restaurants, or small shops for refreshments. Larger towns like Maceda itself will offer more options. It's recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer or more remote routes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The Maceda – tour around Baños de Molgas route is approximately 25.4 km long and generally takes about 1 hour and 39 minutes to complete by touring bicycle. This duration can vary based on your cycling pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or points of interest.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While it offers scenic beauty, expect rolling hills and varied terrain. It's suitable for cyclists with a reasonable level of fitness, and some sections might include unpaved or more challenging surfaces, typical of the Ourense region.

faqs.question: Is the Maceda – tour around Baños de Molgas route a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is designed as a circular tour, starting and ending in the same general area, allowing you to explore the surroundings of Maceda and Baños de Molgas without needing to arrange a return trip.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: You can expect a mix of terrain, characteristic of the Ourense province. The route will likely feature rolling hills, with both well-paved sections and potentially some unpaved parts. The area is rich in natural landscapes, including rivers and forests, offering a diverse cycling experience.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The tour offers several notable highlights. You can visit the historic Castillo de Maceda, enjoy the facilities and natural beauty around Baños de Molgas Spa and Arnoia River, and see the impressive Sanctuary of Our Lady of Miracles.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region of Galicia, where this route is located, offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder, and the natural landscapes are particularly vibrant, making for a more enjoyable experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: As the route connects Maceda and Baños de Molgas, you can typically find parking in either municipality. Look for public parking areas within Maceda or Baños de Molgas, which serve as convenient starting points for your tour.

faqs.question: Is the Maceda – tour around Baños de Molgas route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many natural areas in Galicia are dog-friendly, specific regulations for cycling routes can vary. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in populated areas or near wildlife. Always ensure your dog is comfortable with the distance and terrain before bringing them on a moderate cycling tour.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required for cycling on public roads and established routes in this region of Ourense. However, if you plan to visit specific attractions like the Castillo de Maceda or the Baños de Molgas Spa, they may have their own entry fees or operating hours.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Maceda and Baños de Molgas are municipalities with local amenities. You can expect to find cafes, restaurants, and other facilities in these towns where you can refuel and refresh during or after your cycling tour.

faqs.question: What kind of natural landscapes will I see?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through the beautiful natural landscapes of Ourense, characterized by rolling hills, native forests, and the presence of rivers like the Arnoia. You'll experience traditional Galician scenery, offering a peaceful and picturesque backdrop for your ride.
